title: Hadoop MapReduce
description:
Hadoop MapReduce is a software framework for easily writing applications which process vast amounts of data (multi-terabyte data-sets) parallelly on large clusters (thousands of nodes).
- The system is divided into two main components: the Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS) and MapReduce.
- HDFS provides the distributed storage for the system, while MapReduce provides the parallel processing of data across the cluster.
- The system is designed to scale up from single servers to thousands of machines, each offering local computation and storage.
- MapReduce is based on the concept of mapping data to a set of intermediate key-value pairs, followed by a reduce step to aggregate the values to a smaller set of output key-value pairs.
- The system is fault-tolerant and provides high-throughput, low-latency processing of data.
- The system is written in Java and uses the Google protobuf data serialization format.

TITLE: Hadoop MapReduce

DESCRIPTION:
Hadoop MapReduce is a software framework for easily writing applications which process vast amounts of data (multi-terabyte data-sets) parallelly on large clusters (thousands of nodes).
- The system is divided into two main components: the Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS) and MapReduce.
- HDFS provides the distributed storage for the system, while MapReduce provides the parallel processing of data across the cluster.
- The system is designed to scale up from single servers to thousands of machines, each offering local computation and storage.
- MapReduce is based on the concept of mapping data to a set of intermediate key-value pairs, followed by a reduce step to aggregate the values to a smaller set of output key-value pairs.
- The system is fault-tolerant and provides high-throughput, low-latency processing of data.
- The system is written in Java and uses the Google protobuf data serialization format.
